steps.title: Identify what comes before and after the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: "contains three signature elements of Gwendolyn Brooks's poetic ___" — a complete independent clause announcing a list. After the blank: three list items beginning with "terse,". A colon is the conventional mark that introduces a list or elaboration after a complete independent clause.
  • Choice A — 'style. Terse,': A period ends the sentence, leaving "Terse, compressed lines..." as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'style, terse,': A comma alone joining a clause to a list creates a comma splice; no list-signaling punctuation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'style: terse,': A colon after the independent clause correctly introduces the three-item list that follows. ✓
  • Choice D — 'style; terse,': A semicolon must separate two independent clauses; "terse, compressed lines..." is not an independent clause. ✗

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: Colon = complete clause before, list or elaboration after. The blank sits exactly at that boundary. That matches choice C — C is correct.
